name: AMPK signaling
notes: Energy-sensing kinase; activated by low ATP/high AMP. Counter-balances mTOR. Implicated in autism mitochondrial subtype and as metformin's primary target.

name: IGF-1 / insulin signaling
notes: Growth-factor axis. Low IGF-1 in autism cohorts; IGF-1 trials in Rett (MECP2) and Phelan-McDermid (SHANK3) ongoing.

name: FOXO transcription factors
notes: Stress-response and longevity TFs. Regulate autophagy, antioxidant defense, synaptic plasticity. Counter-regulated by IGF-1/AKT.

name: SIRTUIN / NAD+ metabolism
notes: NAD+-dependent deacetylases. SIRT1/3 implicated in mitochondrial biogenesis and neuronal protection. NAD+ levels reportedly low in autism subsets.

name: PI3K/AKT signaling
notes: Central growth/survival kinase axis. Hyperactivation in PTEN/TSC syndromic autism. Drives mTOR upstream.

name: HPA axis dysregulation
notes: Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis. Elevated baseline cortisol and blunted reactivity reported in autism. Linked to maternal stress transmission and vagal tone.

name: Mast cell activation
notes: MCAS overlaps with autism in subset; histamine/tryptase release drives BBB permeability and neuroinflammation.

name: Endocannabinoid system
notes: CB1/CB2 modulation of glutamate, GABA, microglial activation. Anandamide low in some autism cohorts; CBD trials positive.

name: Vagus nerve / autonomic regulation
notes: Vagal tone regulates inflammation (cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway), gut-brain signaling, social engagement (polyvagal theory). Reduced HRV variability reported in autism.

name: Calcium / glutamate-NMDA homeostasis
notes: Excitotoxicity hypothesis; NMDA hyperactivation; calcium dysregulation in mitochondria. Targeted by memantine, riluzole, magnesium.

name: Short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) signaling
notes: Butyrate, acetate, propionate produced by Faecalibacterium, Roseburia, Eubacterium. Modulate microglial maturation, BBB integrity, T-reg differentiation. Both protective (low dose) and toxic (high propionate animal models).

name: Lipopolysaccharide (LPS) endotoxemia / leaky gut
notes: Increased intestinal permeability allows LPS translocation; TLR4 activation drives systemic + neuroinflammation. Reported elevated in autism cohorts.

name: Tryptophan-kynurenine metabolism
notes: Microbial diversion of tryptophan to kynurenine pathway (reducing serotonin synthesis) and to indoles (AhR signaling). Altered ratios reported in autism.

name: p-cresol / 4-EPS aromatic metabolite production
notes: Clostridia produce p-cresol from tyrosine; reaches brain, inhibits dopamine-β-hydroxylase. 4-EPS produced by Bacteroides fragilis-deficient gut induces autism-like behavior in mice (Hsiao 2013).

name: Bile acid metabolism / FXR signaling
notes: Microbial deconjugation and 7α-dehydroxylation of primary bile acids; secondary bile acids signal via FXR/TGR5 to regulate metabolism, inflammation, neuronal function.

name: Microbial GABA / neurotransmitter synthesis
notes: Specific Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium strains produce GABA, serotonin precursors, dopamine. Bifidobacterium-deficient autism cohorts may have reduced microbial GABA contribution.

name: Heat shock protein (HSF1/HSP70/HSP90) response
notes: Cellular stress-response chaperone system. Refolds misfolded proteins, prevents aggregation, modulates inflammation. Induced by heat (sauna), cold, exercise, sulforaphane. Hypothesized mechanism for the 'fever effect' in autism (Curran 2007).

name: BDNF / neurotrophin signaling
notes: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor; central to synaptic plasticity, neurogenesis, dendritic growth. Reduced serum BDNF in autism subsets. Lactate-driven by exercise; modulated by ketones, sulforaphane, omega-3.

name: Vitamin-D-regulated serotonin synthesis (TPH2)
notes: Calcitriol activates TPH2 transcription via VDRE in brain (serotonin↑) and represses TPH1 in periphery. Patrick & Ames 2014: explains low brain / high peripheral serotonin paradox in autism. Distinct from kynurenine pathway (MEC-0023).

name: Autophagy / proteostasis
notes: Cellular quality-control: degradation of damaged proteins and organelles. Activated by fasting, exercise, caloric restriction, rapamycin (mTOR inhibition), AMPK signaling. Disrupted in syndromic autism (TSC, PTEN — mTOR hyperactivation). Distinct from mTOR pathway itself (MEC-0009) — autophagy is downstream effector.

name: DHA membrane phospholipid composition
notes: DHA (22:6n-3) incorporated into neuronal phospholipids (phosphatidylserine, phosphatidylethanolamine). Determines membrane fluidity, receptor function, signaling. Phospholipid DHA accumulates ~10x more in brain than triglyceride DHA (Werner 2011). Distinct from neuroinflammation effects.
